code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test16-SEP-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and potassium, suggesting impaired renal function. Marked proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria with low urine specific gravity indicate possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy. Cloudy, brown urine with ammoniacal odor may reflect underlying renal pathology rather than infection (no leukocytes/nitrites). Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for diabetes and renal disease.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
